metadata.title: Summits and Conferences in Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Which ministry organised the Rising Northeast Investors Summit 2025?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

answer.label: C
answer.text: Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (MDoNER) organized the Rising Northeast Investors Summit 2025 to boost trade and investment in the North Eastern Region. The summit focused on attracting investments in sectors such as tourism and hospitality, agro-food processing and allied areas, textiles, handloom and handicrafts, healthcare, education and skill development, entertainment and sports, infrastructure and logistics, and energy. The summit, along with pre-event roadshows, attracted investment interests worth ₹ 4.48 lakh crore.

questions.question: Which country is the host of Artificial Intelligence (AI) Impact Summit 2026?
questions.explanation: India will host the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Impact Summit in New Delhi from February 15 to 20, 2026. The summit aims to shape the future of inclusive, responsible, and resilient Artificial Intelligence (AI). It will provide a platform to bridge gaps in technology and AI adoption. The event is guided by three principles: People, Planet, and Progress, focusing on serving humanity, protecting the environment, and driving inclusive growth. A Research Symposium on AI will be held to bring together researchers and practitioners for idea exchange, collaboration, and presenting AI’s impact.

questions.question: Where was Rajasthan Regional AI Impact Conference 2026 inaugurated?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is hosting the Regional AI Impact Conference 2026 on 6th January in Jaipur. The event is held in collaboration with Rajasthan DigiFest and TiE Global Summit 2026, leading up to the India AI Impact Summit 2026 in February. The conference explores how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can drive governance reform, economic growth, innovation, and inclusive development. Sessions include AI for Public Service Delivery, Ethical AI, AI and Future of Employment & Skills, and Rajasthan’s AI startup ecosystem.

questions.question: What is the theme of the National Textiles Ministers’ Conference 2026?

options.label: A
options.text: Textiles for Tomorrow: Growth and Sustainability

options.label: B
options.text: India’s Textiles: Weaving Growth, Heritage & Innovation

options.label: D
options.text: Handlooms, Handicrafts and Heritage of India

answer.label: B
answer.text: India’s Textiles: Weaving Growth, Heritage & Innovation

questions.explanation: The National Textiles Ministers’ Conference 2026 has been inaugurated in Guwahati, Assam, organised by the Ministry of Textiles in collaboration with the Government of Assam. The theme of the two-day conference is “India’s Textiles: Weaving Growth, Heritage & Innovation”. It brings together Textile Ministers and senior officials from States and Union Territories to deliberate on policy, investment, sustainability, exports, infrastructure, and technological advancement in textiles. The conference aims to strengthen Centre–State collaboration and create a roadmap for a competitive, sustainable, and inclusive textile sector.

questions.question: Where was third International Conference on Indian Languages 2026 held?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President of India, Shri C. P. Radhakrishnan, inaugurated the Third International Conference on Indian Languages in New Delhi. The Conference was organised by Vaishvik Hindi Parivaar, Antarashtriya Sahyog Parishad, Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts, and the Department of Bharatiya Bhasha, University of Delhi. Indian languages have strengthened national unity and preserved a shared civilisational ethos and common Dharma. The Eighth Schedule of the Constitution recognises India’s linguistic diversity, affirming that national unity depends on mutual respect, not uniformity.

questions.question: Which country is the host of 28th Conference of Speakers and Presiding Officers of the Commonwealth (CSPOC)?
questions.explanation: India is hosting the 28th Conference of Speakers and Presiding Officers of the Commonwealth (CSPOC) in New Delhi from 14–16 January 2026. The three-day conference brings together Speakers and Presiding Officers from over 60 Commonwealth countries and semi-autonomous Parliaments. The aim is to promote knowledge and understanding of parliamentary democracy and develop parliamentary institutions. Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla is the Chairperson of the 28th Conference of Speakers and Presiding Officers of the Commonwealth (CSPOC).

questions.question: Where was National Conference on Strategic Trade Controls (NCSTC) 2026 held?
questions.explanation: The National Conference on Strategic Trade Controls (NCSTC) 2026 was organised in New Delhi to strengthen India’s Strategic Trade Control framework for regulating exports of sensitive and dual-use items. The conference focused on the SCOMET (Special Chemicals, Organisms, Materials, Equipment and Technologies) framework and its role in preventing proliferation risks while supporting legitimate trade. The conference also addressed export control challenges in emerging and frontier technologies such as semiconductors, quantum technology, aerospace, biotechnology and cyber security.

questions.question: Where was a two day conference on “Enhancing Gender Statistics” organised?
questions.explanation: Recently, a two-day conference on “Enhancing Gender Statistics” was organised by the Social Statistics Division, Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) and National Council of Applied Economic Research (NCAER) on 15–16 January 2026 at New Delhi. The conference brought together policymakers, researchers, and statisticians to discuss improving gender-responsive data systems.

questions.question: Where was 86th All India Presiding Officers Conference (AIPOC) 2026 held?
questions.explanation: The 86th All India Presiding Officers Conference was held in Lucknow at Uttar Pradesh Vidhan Sabha. The three-day conference (19-21 January) brings together presiding officers, secretaries, and representatives to discuss legislative procedures, parliamentary traditions, House conduct, good governance, and current legislative issues. Key objectives include leveraging technology for transparent, efficient, and citizen-centric legislative processes, capacity building of legislators to improve efficiency, strengthening democratic governance, and enhancing accountability of the legislature towards the public.

questions.question: Which city is the host of 2nd Global Buddhist Summit 2026?
questions.explanation: The 2nd Global Buddhist Summit is scheduled to take place in January 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. It is organised by the International Buddhist Confederation (IBC) with the Ministry of Culture, Government of India. The theme is “Collective Wisdom, United Voice, Mutual Coexistence”. The summit aims to promote Buddhist values like compassion, wisdom, harmony, and peace.
